rulings.Case Title: BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. ET AL VS. NKECHI EMERUWA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Budget Employee Kintresa Banks And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for September 4, 2025, line 1. PLAINTIFF NKECHI EMERUWA-NEUBERG'S (FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE 593982) Motion To Compel Deposition Of Budget Employee Kintresa Banks And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Counsel. Nkechi Emeruwa's motion to compel deposition of Kintresa Banks is DENIED. Emeruwa has failed to show good cause for an order compelling the deposition of Banks. Emeruwa and her counsel's conduct does not demonstrate a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the issues. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040.) The parties were still in the process of choosing a date for Banks' deposition. Emeruwa's counsel's declaration shows that the parties were in the process of scheduling Banks' deposition when Emeruwa abruptly and unreasonably imposed a three-day deadline for providing dates. The scheduling of this deposition was also concurrent with Emeruwa herself failing to appear for her court-ordered deposition on June 10, 2025. It would be reasonable for Budget to prioritize efforts to depose Emeruwa before working out the date of Banks' employee deposition. Counsel for Emeruwa's declaration shows no flexibility in scheduling this deposition after requesting flexibility from the other side when Emeruwa missed her deposition only weeks prior. Thus, the motion is denied. The court finds Emeruwa acted without substantial justification as to this discovery and this motion. Emeruwa shall pay $3,360 to Budget Rent A Car System Inc. as sanctions forthwith but no later than September 25, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010(h) & 2023.030(a).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RUBEN MARTINEZ VS. SFHD PARTNERS, LLC DBA SAN FRANCISCO HARLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant M. Rawson To Respond To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for September 4, 2025, line 3. PLAINTIFF RUBEN MARTINEZ'S Motion To Compel Defendant M. Rawson To Respond To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Ruben Martinez's Motion To Compel Defendant Michael Rawson's Response To Plaintiff's Demand For Production Of Documents And Things, Set No. One, and for Sanctions is GRANTED. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) The record demonstrates Defendant Michael Rawson did not provide any responses to the RFPs. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for requests for production of documents when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300.) Defendant Michael Rawson shall serve verified, objection-free, responses to Plaintiff's RFPs, Set One by September 25, 2025. Defendant Michael Rawson did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Michael Rawson shall pay $860 to Plaintiffs as sanctions, payment no later than September 25, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure 2031.300(c).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: KELLY JONES ET AL VS. WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for September 4, 2025, line 5. PLAINTIFFS KELLY JONES, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS, DIAMOND QUAN, CHRISTINA ZINZUN'S Request For Dismissal [C.R.C. 3.770]. Plaintiffs Kelly Jones Diamond Quan and Christina Zinzun's unopposed request for dismissal California Rules of Court, rule 3.770 is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiffs seek a court order under California Rules of Court rule 3.770. Plaintiff does not provide sufficient information or analysis. "Requests for dismissal must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the facts on which the party relies. The declaration must clearly state whether consideration, direct or indirect, is being given for the dismissal and must describe the consideration in detail." (California Rules of Court, rule 3.770(a).) The Marker Declaration does not describe the consideration given at all, let alone "in detail." Nor does the Marker Declaration clearly state whether consideration, direct or indirect, is being given for dismissal of the class claims (even if without prejudice). Nor is there any analysis in the memorandum of points and authorities demonstrating Plaintiffs received no consideration for dismissal of the class claims, which generally will require an analysis of the individual claims, their value, the settlement amount, the relation between these figures; the attorney's fees recovery and whether it demonstrates consideration for abandoning the class claims. The court's adjudication will likely focus on the specific terms of the settlement agreement; at least the relevant provisions must be before the court and part of the public record on the motion. The court declines the parties' invitation to review the settlement without public access. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ENRIQUE OSVALDO CAYAZZO VS. FLAGSHIP FACILITY SERVICES LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for September 4, 2025, line 11. DEFENDANT FLAGSHIP FACILITY SERVICES LLC'S Amended MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendant Flagship Facility Services LLC's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The court's tenative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
